content_markdown:
The graph shows the variation with absolute temperature $T$ of the pressure $p$ of a fixed mass of an ideal gas.

Which of the following is correct concerning the volume and the density of the gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A graph of pressure p versus absolute temperature T showing a straight line passing through the origin (0,0). The line is dashed near the origin and solid as it increases.

vibe_explanation: According to the ideal gas law $pV = nRT$, if the graph of $p$ against $T$ is a straight line through the origin, then $p/T$ is constant. This implies that $V$ must be constant. Since the mass of the gas is fixed and density is mass divided by volume, a constant volume means the density must also remain constant.
